meaning: adj.个人的n.有利害关系的人
examples: She was not privy to any information contained in the letters.
Only three people, including a policeman, will be privy to the facts.
I'm not privy to any pending announcement, so calm down.
Its proposals are open for comment on the Privy Council website until 23 May.
I've not been privy to all the advice and experts that he's heard from.
